title: A Thought on Beautiful World
wikitext:
[]: A Thought on "Beautiful World"             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 31, 2007 5:56 pm The song uses the phrase "Boku no sekai". I don't know much Japanese, but I'm pretty sure that particular phrase means "My world". However, the singer is female. Isn't "boku" a supposedly masculine word? Is the song, despite the singer, supposed to be from the perspective of a male?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 31, 2007 6:10 pm Boku is used a lot in songs, even when clearly from the PoV of a woman; probably for reason of cadence, rather than being tomboyish.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Wed Oct 31, 2007 6:18 pm Methinks it was just to get the syllables to match. WA-TA-SHI-NO-SE-KA-I has one more syllable than the other lines to go with it...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 31, 2007 7:15 pm It's a song that's either FROM Shinji's perspective or about Shinji. (The translations of it floating on the Net seems to support this, I think.) Especially these lines (translated): "Sleeping or awake, Boy's manga. Only dreaming, I don't like myself. I don't know what I want, but I want it. A tear travels across my cheek Another line a bit later: "I can't say what I want, I don't have any guts. But that's fine."

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 31, 2007 8:49 pm I can see it as being from Shinji's perspective, except this: does Shinji really consider the world (or himself) beautiful? Is the "beautiful boy" Kaworu? XD

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Oct 31, 2007 9:59 pm I thought the song was sung from differnet perspectives, there is a line in there where someone goes you don't know how beuftfuil you are...put boy somewhre in there. I'd like to sleep next to you anywhere would be fine. While some pharses are what Shinji would say, like I don't like myself. It has to be either Misato (M/S shippers delight) or Rei (EWW your mom has the hots for you gross) or Asuka even...though since she dosen't premeire in this film...I really doubt it.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 31, 2007 10:03 pm NAveryW wrote: I can see it as being from Shinji's perspective, except this: does Shinji really consider the world (or himself) beautiful? Is the "beautiful boy" Kaworu? XD

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:03 am I bet the lyricist didn't know anything about the movie. They could have made "Beautiful World" the theme song for any number of other anime and people would be able to argue over how it applied to the main character. That is to say: Vague lyrics are vaaaaaaaaaaague.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:14 am Actually, it fits Byosoku go Senchimeetoru far better than Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:59 am I can confirm Boku is a mostly masculine term. This song could be from Mistao's . . . wait, she is clearly a female. And the translated lyrics make her seem like a pedo if it was from her perspective. Maybe from Shinji's . . . does he really think the world is beautiful? You know what? Maybe it is Asuka. Hehs, I mean, I guess she doesn't appear in the movie, but still . . . um . . . yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 01, 2007 3:55 am If anyone else here watched Hataraki Man (and you ought to have, being the anime of the manga by Moyoco Anno), you'll see that its OP song is from the point of view of Hiroko, is sung by a woman, and uses boku -- even thought Hiroko is no bokuko, despite her "working man" mode. (And that's just the first and most appropriate example that comes to mind.)

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Nov 01, 2007 7:17 am Mr. Tines wrote: If anyone else here watched Hataraki Man (and you ought to have, being the anime of the manga by Moyoco Anno), you'll see that its OP song is from the point of view of Hiroko, is sung by a woman, and uses boku -- even thought Hiroko is no bokuko, despite her "working man" mode. (And that's just the first and most appropriate example that comes to mind.)

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Thu Nov 01, 2007 8:33 am Brrrrr... Read the comments HERE All explained... p.s. Sasai no giron wo YAMERO! Syaku ni sawatte yo...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 01, 2007 10:30 am CitizenGeek wrote: I know nothing of Japanese, really, but is it possible that "boku" refers to "sekai", sekai being a masculine noun and therefore requiring a masculine possesion word to precede it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 01, 2007 11:52 am CitizenGeek wrote: I know nothing of Japanese, really, but is it possible that ...

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:06 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Japanese is as unlike any Indo-European language as you can get. It has almost none of the grammatical constructs that we recognise (tenses, gender, plurals); but rather slices up its map of reality along very different lines.

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:14 pm ざけんじゃねーよ,イカめ!クン・フーが上手だ! ははは、ジャストキッディング!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:26 pm CtHuLhU wrote: 日本語

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 01, 2007 1:31 pm Shinji uses the term "boku" when talking about himself. Actually, I think he's one of the few characters in Eva that do. (Touji uses washi, Kensuke ore, Kaji usually ore, a few times boku, Gendou & Fuyutsuki: Watashi. The female characters: Either Watashi or Atashi, some use both (e.g. Asuka)) Hence why I think it's a song that's either about him or from his perspective. And NAveryW: Shinji doesn't consider himself to be beautiful. That's part of the song's point.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Nov 02, 2007 5:07 pm CtHuLhU wrote: 少女よ休日になれ!!wwwwww

I don't know much Japanese, but I'm pretty sure that particular phrase means "My world". However, the singer is female. Isn't "boku" a supposedly masculine word? Is the song, despite the singer, supposed to be from the perspective of a male?
